metaTitle: Image Resizer — Resize Photos for Instagram, YouTube, Twitter (Free & Private)
metaDescription: Resize images instantly in your browser with SNS presets or custom dimensions. No upload required — 100% private. Supports JPG, PNG, WebP with high-quality scaling.

howto.description: Click the upload area or drag and drop an image (JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF). Your file stays securely on your device.
Choose from popular SNS presets like Instagram Post, YouTube Thumbnail, or TikTok Video. Or, enter your own custom width and height.
Keep the 'Lock Aspect Ratio' toggle on to ensure your image doesn't look stretched or distorted while resizing.
Click 'Resize Image' to process. Your perfectly sized file will be ready for instant download.

why: 100% private — your images are processed in your browser and never uploaded
Ready-to-use SNS presets for Instagram, Twitter, TikTok, YouTube, and Facebook
Advanced scaling algorithms (Pica) for high-quality results with minimal blur
Custom dimension controls with aspect ratio locking for precision
Supports all modern image formats including WebP and transparent PNGs
Works completely offline after the initial page load
Completely free with no watermarks and no registration required

faq.q: Is it safe to resize my personal photos here?
faq.a: Yes. This tool uses client-side processing, meaning your photos are never sent to a server. All resizing happens within your browser's memory, ensuring total privacy for your images.

faq.q: What size should I use for Instagram posts?
faq.a: For square posts, 1080x1080px is best. For portrait, use 1080x1350px. These and many other social media presets are built directly into the tool for your convenience.

faq.q: Will my image lose quality when I resize it?
faq.a: We use the high-performance Pica library for resizing, which uses advanced interpolation to maintain sharpness. However, please note that significantly enlarging a small image may result in some loss of clarity.

faq.q: What does 'Lock Aspect Ratio' actually do?
faq.a: It keeps your image's proportions consistent. If you change the width, the height will adjust automatically to prevent your photo from looking squeezed or stretched.

faq.q: Can I resize multiple images at once?
faq.a: To ensure maximum performance and privacy on all devices, this tool currently processes one image at a time. You can quickly resize multiple files by repeating the process.
The tool supports JPG, PNG, WebP, and GIF. It typically maintains your original file format unless you choose otherwise during the export process.
